mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates a robust economic expansion, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and thus positive equity returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has shown consistent growth since 2010.
failure_condition: The signal's information coefficient (IC) is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate over a three-month period signals a cyclical downturn, reducing expected corporate profits and causing negative equity returns as recession fears mount.
evidence_refs: fact:The 'Sahm Rule' uses a similar unemployment trigger for recessions.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation accelerates while the Fed Funds rate remains stable or falls, it signals the central bank is 'behind the curve'. This increases risk premia and expectations of future, more aggressive tightening, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The signal has a positive information coefficient over a 5-year period, indicating it incorrectly predicts market direction.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery environment characterized by low interest rates, accelerating retail sales growth confirms the expansion's durability, boosting investor confidence and driving positive short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is the largest component of US GDP.
failure_condition: The signal's performance is not statistically different (p > 0.1) inside vs. outside of periods where FEDFUNDS is below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in industrial production growth indicates weakening manufacturing and business investment, leading to downward revisions in earnings forecasts and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The hit rate of the signal (correctly predicting negative returns) is below 50% over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in housing starts reflects strong consumer balance sheets and builder confidence, acting as a leading indicator for broader economic activity and positively impacting equity markets over the very short term.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing has a large multiplier effect on the economy.
failure_condition: The signal's 5-day forward return Sharpe ratio is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a zero-interest-rate policy (ZIRP) regime, any meaningful acceleration in CPI inflation increases the probability of a hawkish policy shift from the central bank, compressing equity valuations and causing negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Decision date 2014-06-15 is within the ZIRP era.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return is positive when the trigger condition is met over the 2010-2015 period.

mechanism_narrative: A higher-than-expected real GDP growth rate confirms broad economic momentum, leading to upward revisions of corporate earnings across the market and supporting positive equity returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 is the broadest measure of economic output.
failure_condition: The correlation between quarterly real GDP growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive (p > 0.10) over a 10-year sample.
